zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless於1506年出生在英國英格蘭肯特時，她的父親Sir William Lovelace II26歲，母親Alice Mae Stevens Shawe6歲。她於1522年與Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury在英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托聖 加也 普利奧利 邱奇結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1599年5月15日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾享年93歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless于1506年出生在英国英格兰肯特时，她的父亲Sir William Lovelace II26岁，母亲Alice Mae Stevens Shawe6岁。她于1522年与Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury在英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托聖 加也 普利奧利 邱奇结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1599年5月15日在英国英格兰史洛普爾享年93岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾。

nameMeanings.name: Mynott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Minett
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from Old French mignot, Anglo-Norman French minot, mignot, minnot ‘elegant, gracious, kind, courteous’. Compare French Mignot .
Altered form of French   Minet  . Compare   Minette  .

nameMeanings.name: Loveless
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English loveles ‘loveless, without love’, probably in the sense ‘fancy free’.
some early examples, such as Richard Lovelas (Kent 1344), may have as their second element Middle English las(se) ‘girl’, ‘maiden’.

variants.name: Lovelace
meanings: English: variant of   Loveless  . The spelling is apparently theresult of folk etymology, which understood the word as a nickname fora dandy fond of lace. The modern sense of this word is, however, notattested until the 16th century and at the time of surname formationit meant only ‘cord’ or ‘shoelace’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless was born in 1506, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William Lovelace II, was 26 and her mother, Alice Mae Stevens Shawe, was 6. She married Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury in 1522, in St James' Priory Church, Bristol, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 15 May 1599, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 93, and was buried in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless est née en 1506, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Lovelace II, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alice Mae Stevens Shawe, avait 6 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury en 1522, à St James' Priory Church, Bristol, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 15 mai 1599, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 93 ans, et a été enterrée à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless 1506 in Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Lovelace II 26 und ihre Mutter Alice Mae Stevens Shawe 6. Sie hat 1522 in St James' Priory Church, Bristol, Gloucestershire, England Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 15. Mai 1599 im Alter von 93 Jahren in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless è nata il nel 1506 nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir William Lovelace II, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Alice Mae Stevens Shawe, aveva 6 anni. ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Jane Mynott Lovelessが1506年にイギリスイギリスケントで生まれた時，父Sir William Lovelace IIは26歳，母Alice Mae Stevens Shaweは6歳であった。1522年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストルスト ジャムス' プリオリー チャーチでRobert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterburyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1599年5月15日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで93歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless(이)가 1506년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Lovelace II(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Mae Stevens Shawe(은)는 6세였다. 그녀는 1522년 영국 그루스스터실르 브리스톨 스트 자메스' 프리오리 c후르치에서 Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1599년 5월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 93세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless nasceu em 1506, em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Lovelace II, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Alice Mae Stevens Shawe, tinha 6 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury em 1522, em St James' Priory Church, Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 15 de maio de 1599, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 93 anos, e foi sepultada em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St James, Bristol, Gloucester, England: ст джемес' приори чурч, бристол, глоусестиршир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless родилась в 1506 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir William Lovelace II было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Mae Stevens Shawe, 6. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury в 1522 г. в местности ст джемес' приори чурч, бристол, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 15 мая 1599 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 93 лет и была похоронена в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.St James, Bristol, Gloucester, England: St James' Priory Church, Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Jane Mynott Loveless nació en 1506, en Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Lovelace II, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Alice Mae Stevens Shawe, tenía 6 años. Se casó con Robert Lewis, Alderman, Mayor of Canterbury en 1522, en St James' Priory Church, Bristol, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 15 de mayo de 1599, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 93 años, y fue sepultada en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
